source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The mechanism shown in figure is used to raise a crate of supplies from a ship's hold. The crate has total mass \(50\text{ kg}\). A rope is wrapped around a wooden cylinder that turns on a metal axle. The cylinder has radius \(0.25\text{ m}\) and moment of inertia \(I = 2.9\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2\) about the axle. The crate is suspended from the free end of the rope. One end of the axle pivots on frictionless bearings; a crank handle is attached to the other end. When the crank is turned, the end of the handle rotates about the axle in a vertical circle, the cylinder turns, and the crate is raised. What magnitude of the force \(\vec{F}\) applied tangentially to the rotating crank is required to raise the crate with an acceleration of \(1.40\text{ m/s}^2\)? (You can ignore the mass of the rope as well as the moments of inertia of the axle and the crank.)

image_dependency: Question explicitly references a figure: 'The mechanism shown in figure'.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is registered as image-reliant.
The text gives cylinder radius but does not state the crank-handle radius/geometry, so a key parameter for the applied force likely comes from the figure.
multi_step: Solving requires combining translational motion of the 50 kg crate with rotational motion of the cylinder/crank system.
The problem provides multiple physical quantities (mass, radius, moment of inertia, acceleration) that must be related through more than one equation.
The target quantity is an applied tangential force on the crank, which typically requires chaining tension, angular acceleration, and torque relationships.
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the provided answer is a specific numeric value ('1300N').
The item also includes 4 multiple-choice options, giving an additional discrete verification target.
The question specifies concrete numerical inputs, so the result can be checked by a physics solver if the figure-supplied geometry is available.

答案为可核验的具体短文本数值，且题目给出明确物理量，整体可验证性较好。
